color_semantics.TEAL: quantum correlation / CHSH score 2вИЪ2 / entangled singlet / quantum zone
color_semantics.CRIMSON: local hidden-variable / classical ceiling 2 / pre-set answers / Einstein picture

metadata.note: Card 09 вАФ score 8/10 вАФ scan move вАФ exclusions: no full CHSH algebraic derivation, no loophole taxonomy, no interpretation debate, no Tsirelson-bound proof

beats.narration_text: Einstein believed particles carry hidden pre-set answers before measurement. That belief has a testable consequence вАФ a number that cannot exceed 2.

card.headline: Why 2 Becomes 2вИЪ2 вАФ How Bell Beat Einstein

beats.narration_text: Alice and Bob each choose between two spin-measurement axes. The CHSH score combines four correlations. Any world with pre-set answers: score at most 2. That is the classical ceiling.
beats.description: Two detector icons (Alice, Bob) each with two axis choices. Equation: |E(aвВБ,bвВБ)+E(aвВБ,bвВВ)+E(aвВВ,bвВБ)вИТE(aвВВ,bвВВ)| вЙ§ 2. CRIMSON border around the bound '2'.

beats.narration_text: The question: if every experiment produces a score above 2, what does that mean for Einstein's picture?

card.headline: Can experiment push the score above 2?

beats.narration_text: For the singlet вАФ two entangled spin-half particles вАФ the quantum correlation between axes at angle ќЄ is minus cosine ќЄ. Choose the four axes at 0¬∞, 45¬∞, 90¬∞, 135¬∞ and compute.
beats.description: Four angle labels: aвВБ=0¬∞, bвВБ=45¬∞, aвВВ=90¬∞, bвВВ=135¬∞. Correlation formula: E_QM = вИТcosќЄ in TEAL. Each pairwise angle difference labeled.

beats.narration_text: Each correlation is minus one over root 2. Combine four of them: the CHSH score is 2 root 2 вАФ about 2.83. That is 41 percent above the classical ceiling.
beats.description: Step: each E = вИТ1/вИЪ2 вЙИ вИТ0.707. CHSH sum = вИТ4/вИЪ2 = вИТ2вИЪ2. |S| = 2вИЪ2 вЙИ 2.828. TEAL highlighted. Classical 2 shown below in CRIMSON. Gap labeled '41%'.

beats.narration_text: A classical correlated pair gives linear correlations. The singlet's quantum correlation follows minus cosine ќЄ вАФ a curve, not a line. The curve is more extreme near 45¬∞, and that is exactly where CHSH probes it.
beats.description: Plot: x-axis angle ќЄ from 0¬∞ to 180¬∞. Classical (linear, CRIMSON dashed) vs quantum (вИТcosќЄ curve, TEAL solid). At ќЄ=45¬∞ gap is visible. CHSH probing angles marked with teal dots.

beats.narration_text: Aspect measured 2.697 in 1982. Loophole-free tests in 2015 confirmed 2.828 at ten standard deviations. Local hidden variables are ruled out, not just unlikely.
beats.description: Number line: 0 to 3. Marker at 2 (CRIMSON, 'LHV max'). Marker at 2вИЪ2вЙИ2.83 (TEAL, 'Tsirelson bound / quantum max'). Arrow at 2.697 labeled 'Aspect 1982'. Arrow at 2.828 labeled '2015 loophole-free'.

beats.narration_text: The Tsirelson bound shows 2 root 2 is the highest any quantum state can score вАФ no quantum trick can go further. Between 2 and 2 root 2 sits everything quantum mechanics allows but local hidden variables forbid.

props.headline: The Tsirelson bound shows 2 root 2 is the highest anyвА¶

beats.still_prompt: B10, clean educational diagram: a horizontal number line from 0 to 3. At value 2: a red vertical bar labeled 'Classical ceiling (LHV вЙ§ 2)'. At value 2.828: a teal vertical bar labeled 'Tsirelson / Quantum max (2вИЪ2)'. The region between 2 and 2.828 is shaded teal and labeled 'Quantum territory'. To the left of 2 is shaded crimson and labeled 'Classical territory'. Black background, white axis and text labels, clean physics illustration.

beats.narration_text: Entanglement does not allow faster-than-light signaling. Bob's marginal outcome is random regardless of Alice's choice. To see the correlation, you must compare data вАФ and that comparison travels at light speed.
beats.description: Alice and Bob separated by large distance. Alice flips a coin choosing axis. Bob's outcome histogram: same random 50/50 either way. Labels: 'No FTL signal вАФ marginal is random'. Only after classical comparison: correlation appears.

beats.narration_text: Singlet: E = вИТcosќЄ. Angles: aвВБ=0¬∞, bвВБ=45¬∞, aвВВ=90¬∞, bвВВ=135¬∞. EвВБвВБ = вИТcos 45¬∞ = вИТ1/вИЪ2. EвВБвВВ = вИТcos 45¬∞ = вИТ1/вИЪ2. EвВВвВБ = вИТcos 45¬∞ = вИТ1/вИЪ2. EвВВвВВ = вИТcos 135¬∞ = +1/вИЪ2. CHSH sum: 4 times 1/вИЪ2 equals 2вИЪ2.
beats.description: Four-row table: (aвВБ,bвВБ)=45¬∞ вЖТ вИТ1/вИЪ2; (aвВБ,bвВВ)=45¬∞ вЖТ вИТ1/вИЪ2; (aвВВ,bвВБ)=45¬∞ вЖТ вИТ1/вИЪ2; (aвВВ,bвВВ)=135¬∞ вЖТ +1/вИЪ2. Sum shown: |вИТ1/вИЪ2 вИТ1/вИЪ2 вИТ1/вИЪ2 вИТ(+1/вИЪ2)| = 4/вИЪ2 = 2вИЪ2. Final box TEAL.

beats.narration_text: Can experiment push the score above 2? Yes вАФ to exactly 2 root 2. That 41-percent gap is the experimental signature of quantum entanglement, ruling out every local pre-set-answer theory Einstein imagined.

card.headline:
Entanglement scores 2вИЪ2.
Pre-set answers are dead.

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: Part of the Quantum Mechanics A Companion Guide series.
